findings.evidence: Grep hits show the existing reusable API at `Regulator.lean:47` (`regulator_eq_one_of_rank_eq_zero`), `:57` (`rank_eq_zero_of_card_infinitePlace_eq_one`), and `:80` (`rank_eq_zero_of_finrank_eq_one`); the new proofs at `:65-67`, `:72-77`, `:87-88`, `:92-95`, and `:99-100` are direct compositions of those lemmas plus arithmetic.
findings.fix: Remove these parallel regulator wrappers and have callers use `regulator_eq_one_of_rank_eq_zero` composed with `rank_eq_zero_of_card_infinitePlace_eq_one` or `rank_eq_zero_of_finrank_eq_one` as needed.
findings.issue: The new `regulator_eq_one_of_card_infinitePlace_eq_one`, `..._le_one`, `..._finrank_eq_one`, `..._finrank_le_one`, and `..._finrank_lt_two` declarations are special-case wrappers over the existing rank-zero regulator theorem and rank-zero criteria.

summary: The added regulator exact-evaluation wrappers duplicate the existing rank-zero regulator API as small special cases. This creates parallel entry points where callers can already compose the rank-zero evaluation with the existing rank-zero criteria.
